comments:
Viewport — A first-class container for displaying content.

## Philosophy

A Viewport is NOT just "the browser window." It's a **bounded viewing region**
with its own identity, settings, and controls. Think:

- A video player viewport (with playback controls, fullscreen toggle)
- A document viewport (with zoom, scroll position, page navigation)
- A 3D scene viewport (with camera controls, render settings)
- A dashboard viewport (with widget arrangement, refresh interval)
- A code editor viewport (with syntax highlighting, line numbers)
- A split pane viewport (one of N viewports in a layout)

Each viewport has:
- **Identity** (UUID5) — deterministic, reproducible across sessions
- **Dimensions** — current width/height in CSS pixels
- **Settings** — configuration specific to the content type
- **Controls** — actions available to manipulate the viewport
- **Content** — what's being displayed (polymorphic)

## Breakpoints vs Viewport

Breakpoints describe **size thresholds** — they're properties OF a viewport.
A viewport IS the container; breakpoints describe its current state.

## Container Queries

Modern responsive design responds to **container size**, not just window size.
A Viewport naturally supports this — its dimensions are always known,
independent of the browser window.

## Usage

```purescript
import Hydrogen.Schema.Reactive.Viewport as Viewport

-- Create a viewport for a dashboard
dashboardViewport = Viewport.viewport
  { namespace: "acme-corp"
  , name: "main-dashboard"
  , width: 1920
  , height: 1080
  , settings: DashboardSettings { refreshInterval: 30, theme: Dark }
  }

-- Get deterministic ID
Viewport.id dashboardViewport  -- ViewportId "a3f8c2d1-..."

-- Query current breakpoint
Viewport.breakpoint dashboardViewport  -- Xxl

-- Check if viewport supports certain content
Viewport.canDisplay Video dashboardViewport  -- true
```

declarations.comments:
Deterministic identifier for a viewport.

Generated via UUID5 from namespace + name, ensuring the same viewport
configuration always produces the same ID across sessions and systems.
declarations.title: ViewportId

declarations.comments:
Create viewport ID from namespace and name.

```purescript
viewportId "acme-corp" "main-dashboard"
-- => ViewportId "a3f8c2d1-..." (deterministic)
```
declarations.title: viewportId

declarations.comments: Create viewport ID from raw string (for deserialization).
declarations.title: viewportIdFromString

declarations.comments: Unwrap viewport ID to String.
declarations.title: unwrapViewportId

declarations.comments: Convert to string (for serialization).
declarations.title: viewportIdToString

declarations.comments: UUID5 namespace for viewport identities.
declarations.title: viewportNamespace

declarations.comments:
Standard breakpoint tiers.

Aligned with Tailwind CSS breakpoints for ecosystem compatibility.
These are semantic size tiers, not arbitrary pixel values.
declarations.title: Breakpoint

declarations.comments: All breakpoints in order from smallest to largest.
declarations.title: allBreakpoints

declarations.comments: Minimum width (in CSS pixels) where this breakpoint begins.
declarations.title: breakpointMin

declarations.comments:
Maximum width (in CSS pixels) for this breakpoint (exclusive upper bound).
Returns Nothing for Xxl since it has no upper bound.
declarations.title: breakpointMax

declarations.comments: Get the pixel range for a breakpoint.
declarations.title: breakpointRange

declarations.comments: Determine breakpoint from width in CSS pixels.
declarations.title: breakpointFromWidth

declarations.comments: Determine breakpoint and orientation from dimensions.
declarations.title: breakpointFromDimensions

declarations.comments: Screen/viewport orientation.
declarations.title: Orientation

declarations.comments: Determine orientation from aspect ratio (width/height).
declarations.title: orientationFromAspectRatio

declarations.comments: Determine orientation from dimensions.
declarations.title: orientationFromDimensions

declarations.comments:
Semantic device classification.

Higher-level than breakpoints — describes device capabilities,
not just screen size.
declarations.title: DeviceClass

declarations.comments: Map breakpoint to device class.
declarations.title: deviceClassFromBreakpoint

declarations.comments: Get device class from width.
declarations.title: deviceClassFromWidth

declarations.comments:
A viewport — a bounded viewing region with identity and state.

Viewports are first-class containers that can display any content.
They have deterministic identity, dimensions, controls, and settings.
declarations.title: Viewport

declarations.comments:
Create a viewport with auto-generated ID.

ID is derived from namespace + name via UUID5.
declarations.title: viewport

declarations.comments: Create a viewport with explicit ID.
declarations.title: viewportWithId

declarations.comments: Get viewport ID.
declarations.title: getId

declarations.comments: Get viewport width.
declarations.title: getWidth

declarations.comments: Get viewport height.
declarations.title: getHeight

declarations.comments: Get current breakpoint.
declarations.title: getBreakpoint

declarations.comments: Get current orientation.
declarations.title: getOrientation

declarations.comments: Get device class.
declarations.title: getDeviceClass

declarations.comments: Get dimensions as record.
declarations.title: getDimensions

declarations.comments:
Available viewport controls.

Not all viewports support all controls — a document viewer might
support Zoom and Scroll, while a video player supports Fullscreen.
declarations.title: ViewportControl

declarations.comments: Set of available controls for a viewport.
declarations.title: ViewportControls

declarations.comments: Default controls for most viewports.
declarations.title: defaultControls

declarations.comments: Add a control to viewport controls.
declarations.title: addControl

declarations.comments: Remove a control from viewport controls.
declarations.title: removeControl

declarations.comments: Check if viewport has a control.
declarations.title: hasControl

declarations.comments:
Zoom level for viewport content.

1.0 = 100% (fit to viewport)
2.0 = 200% (zoomed in)
0.5 = 50% (zoomed out)
declarations.title: ZoomLevel

declarations.comments: Create zoom level from number.
declarations.title: zoomLevel

declarations.comments: Zoom to fit content in viewport.
declarations.title: zoomFit

declarations.comments: Zoom to fill viewport (may crop).
declarations.title: zoomFill

declarations.comments: 100% zoom.
declarations.title: zoom100

declarations.comments: 50% zoom.
declarations.title: zoom50

declarations.comments: 200% zoom.
declarations.title: zoom200

declarations.comments: Unwrap zoom level to Number.
declarations.title: unwrapZoom

declarations.comments: Mutable state of a viewport.
declarations.title: ViewportState

declarations.comments: Initial viewport state.
declarations.title: initialState

declarations.comments: Set zoom level.
declarations.title: withZoom

declarations.comments: Set scroll position.
declarations.title: withScroll

declarations.comments: Set fullscreen state.
declarations.title: withFullscreen

declarations.comments:
Specification for responsive values.

`base` is required (used for Xs and as fallback).
Other breakpoints are optional — Nothing means "inherit from smaller".
declarations.title: ResponsiveSpec

declarations.comments:
A value that changes at different breakpoints.

Stores resolved value at each breakpoint.
declarations.title: ResponsiveValue

declarations.comments:
Create a responsive value from a spec.

Values cascade up — if a breakpoint is Nothing, it inherits
from the previous smaller breakpoint.
declarations.title: responsive

declarations.comments: Create a static value (same at all breakpoints).
declarations.title: static

declarations.comments: Resolve a responsive value at a specific breakpoint.
declarations.title: resolve

declarations.comments: Resolve a responsive value for a viewport.
declarations.title: resolveFor

declarations.comments:
Generate CSS media query for breakpoint (min-width).

```purescript
mediaQuery Lg  -- "@media (min-width: 1024px)"
mediaQuery Xs  -- "" (no media query for base)
```
declarations.title: mediaQuery

declarations.comments: Generate CSS media query with max-width.
declarations.title: mediaQueryMax

declarations.comments: Generate CSS media query for exact breakpoint range.
declarations.title: mediaQueryRange

declarations.comments:
Generate CSS container query string.

Responds to parent element size, not viewport.
For full container query support, see ContainerQuery module.
declarations.title: containerQueryCss

declarations.comments:
Get Tailwind-style class prefix for breakpoint.

```purescript
breakpointClass Lg "grid-cols-4"  -- "lg:grid-cols-4"
breakpointClass Xs "grid-cols-1"  -- "grid-cols-1"
```
declarations.title: breakpointClass

declarations.comments: Check if breakpoint is at least the threshold.
declarations.title: isAtLeast

declarations.comments: Check if breakpoint is at most the threshold.
declarations.title: isAtMost

declarations.comments: Check if breakpoint is between two thresholds (inclusive).
declarations.title: isBetween

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Reactive.Viewport
